steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: 'Nonetheless' signals a contrast: despite expert advice for complicated passwords, people choose something else. The blank must describe what people actually gravitate toward, as illustrated by '123456789' being the third most common password.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'secure': Contradicts the point — '123456789' is the opposite of secure. ✗
  • Choice B — 'unique': A password used by millions is by definition not unique. ✗
  • Choice C — 'complicated': Contradicts the contrast with expert advice for complicated passwords. ✗
  • Choice D — 'popular': Ranking as the third most commonly used password is direct evidence that people choose it widely, i.e., it is popular. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
